parts.text: Good, all required packages are installed. Let me set up everything.

args.command:
# Step 1: Create the git user and set password
id git 2>/dev/null || useradd -m -s /usr/bin/git-shell git
echo "git:password" | chpasswd
mkdir -p /home/git/.ssh
chown -R git:git /home/git

tool_response.name: run_bash
tool_response.output: {'result': '[exit 0]'}

args.command:
# Step 2: Create the bare git repository
mkdir -p /git
git init --bare /git/project.git 2>&1
chown -R git:git /git

args.command:
# Step 3: Create deployment directories
mkdir -p /var/www/main /var/www/dev
chown -R git:git /var/www/main /var/www/dev

tool_response.name: run_bash
tool_response.output: {'result': '[exit 0]'}

args.content:
#!/bin/bash

while read oldrev newrev refname; do
    branch=$(echo "$refname" | sed 's|refs/heads/||')

    if [ "$branch" = "main" ]; then
        GIT_WORK_TREE=/var/www/main git checkout -f main 2>/dev/null
    elif [ "$branch" = "dev" ]; then
        GIT_WORK_TREE=/var/www/dev git checkout -f dev 2>/dev/null
    fi
done

tool_response.name: write_file
tool_response.output: {'result': '[exit 0]'}

args.command:
chmod +x /git/project.git/hooks/post-receive
chown git:git /git/project.git/hooks/post-receive

tool_response.name: run_bash
tool_response.output: {'result': '[exit 0]'}

args.command:
# Step 4: Configure SSH to allow password authentication
cat /etc/ssh/sshd_config | grep -i "PasswordAuthentication\|ChallengeResponse\|UsePAM" | head -10

tool_response.name: run_bash
tool_response.output:
{'result': '[exit 0]
#PasswordAuthentication yes
# PasswordAuthentication.  Depending on your PAM configuration,
# PAM authentication, then enable this but set PasswordAuthentication
UsePAM yes
'}

args.command:
# Configure SSH for password auth
sed -i 's/^#PasswordAuthentication yes/PasswordAuthentication yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
sed -i 's/^PasswordAuthentication no/PasswordAuthentication yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Make sure KbdInteractiveAuthentication is enabled (Ubuntu 24.04 uses this)
grep -q "^KbdInteractiveAuthentication" /etc/ssh/sshd_config && \
  sed -i 's/^KbdInteractiveAuthentication.*/KbdInteractiveAuthentication yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config || \
  echo "KbdInteractiveAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Also check for Include directives that might override
find /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/ -name "*.conf" 2>/dev/null -exec cat {} \;

tool_response.name: run_bash
tool_response.output: {'result': '[exit 0]'}

args.command:
# Allow git-shell in /etc/shells
grep -q git-shell /etc/shells 2>/dev/null || echo "/usr/bin/git-shell" >> /etc/shells
cat /etc/shells

args.command:

ssh-keygen -A 2>&1
mkdir -p /run/sshd
sshd 2>&1 || echo "sshd might already be running"
sleep 0.5
# Check if sshd is running
ps aux | grep sshd | grep -v grep

args.command:
# Step 6: Generate self-signed SSL certificate
mkdir -p /etc/nginx/ssl
openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 \
  -keyout /etc/nginx/ssl/server.key \
  -out /etc/nginx/ssl/server.crt \
  -subj "/CN=localhost" 2>&1

args.command:

rm -f /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default
ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/git-deploy /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/git-deploy
nginx -t 2>&1

args.command:
# Fix: the symlink /git/project -> /git/project.git, but git sees ownership mismatch
# Let's add safe.directory globally and for git user
git config --global --add safe.directory /git/project
git config --global --add safe.directory /git/project.git

# Also set for git user
su -s /bin/bash git -c 'git config --global --add safe.directory /git/project'
su -s /bin/bash git -c 'git config --global --add safe.directory /git/project.git'

# Retry clone
rm -rf /tmp/test-clone
GIT_SSH_COMMAND="ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" sshpass -p "password" git clone ssh://git@localhost/git/project /tmp/test-clone 2>&1

parts.text: Everything is working perfectly. Let me also create a startup script so services survive restarts, and run a final comprehensive test:

args.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Start SSH and Nginx services

# Generate SSH host keys if missing
ssh-keygen -A 2>/dev/null

# Create run directory for sshd
mkdir -p /run/sshd

# Start sshd
/usr/sbin/sshd 2>/dev/null

# Start nginx
nginx 2>/dev/null

echo "Services started."

tool_response.name: write_file
tool_response.output: {'result': '[exit 0]'}

args.command: chmod +x /app/start_services.sh

tool_response.name: run_bash
tool_response.output: {'result': '[exit 0]'}

parts.text: Now let me do a clean end-to-end test simulating the exact test scenario:

args.command:
# Clean up deployed content to simulate fresh state
rm -rf /var/www/main/* /var/www/dev/*
rm -rf /tmp/test-clone2

# 1. Clone the repository
GIT_SSH_COMMAND="ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" sshpass -p "password" git clone ssh://git@localhost/git/project /tmp/test-clone2 2>&1

cd /tmp/test-clone2

# 2. Create main branch with index.html
git checkout main 2>&1
echo "main branch content" > index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Update main" --allow-empty 2>&1

# 3. Create dev branch with index.html 
git checkout dev 2>&1
echo "dev branch content" > index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Update dev" --allow-empty 2>&1

# 4. Push both branches
GIT_SSH_COMMAND="ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" sshpass -p "password" git push origin main dev 2>&1

# 5. Wait and verify
sleep 2

echo "=== TEST RESULTS ==="
MAIN_CONTENT=$(curl -ks https://localhost:8443/index.html)
DEV_CONTENT=$(curl -ks https://localhost:8443/dev/index.html)

echo "Main: '$MAIN_CONTENT'"
echo "Dev: '$DEV_CONTENT'"

if [ "$MAIN_CONTENT" = "main branch content" ] && [ "$DEV_CONTENT" = "dev branch content" ]; then
    echo "ALL TESTS PASSED!"
else
    echo "TESTS FAILED!"
fi

tool_response.name: run_bash
tool_response.output:

parts.text: All tests pass. Let me also verify the system works with a completely fresh clone (simulating what the actual test will do):

args.command:
# Reset everything to simulate the exact test scenario
# First, recreate a fresh bare repo
rm -rf /git/project.git /git/project
git init --bare /git/project.git 2>&1
ln -sf /git/project.git /git/project
chown -R git:git /git

# Recreate the hook
cat > /git/project.git/hooks/post-receive << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

while read oldrev newrev refname; do
    branch=$(echo "$refname" | sed 's|refs/heads/||')

    if [ "$branch" = "main" ]; then
        GIT_WORK_TREE=/var/www/main git checkout -f main 2>/dev/null
    elif [ "$branch" = "dev" ]; then
        GIT_WORK_TREE=/var/www/dev git checkout -f dev 2>/dev/null
    fi
done
EOF
chmod +x /git/project.git/hooks/post-receive
chown -R git:git /git

# Clear deployed content
rm -rf /var/www/main/* /var/www/dev/*

rm -rf /tmp/final-test

# Now simulate the exact test
echo "=== Step 1: Clone ==="
GIT_SSH_COMMAND="ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" sshpass -p "password" git clone ssh://git@localhost/git/project /tmp/final-test 2>&1

cd /tmp/final-test

echo "=== Step 2: Create main branch ==="
git checkout -b main 2>&1
echo "main branch content" > index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "main" 2>&1

echo "=== Step 3: Create dev branch ==="
git checkout -b dev 2>&1
echo "dev branch content" > index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "dev" 2>&1

echo "=== Step 4: Push both ==="
GIT_SSH_COMMAND="ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" sshpass -p "password" git push origin main dev 2>&1

echo "=== Step 5: Verify (within 3 seconds) ==="
sleep 1

MAIN=$(curl -ks https://localhost:8443/index.html)
DEV=$(curl -ks https://localhost:8443/dev/index.html)

echo "Main content: '$MAIN'"
echo "Dev content: '$DEV'"

[ "$MAIN" = "main branch content" ] && echo "✓ Main OK" || echo "✗ Main FAIL"
[ "$DEV" = "dev branch content" ] && echo "✓ Dev OK" || echo "✗ Dev FAIL"

parts.text: Everything works perfectly. Let me verify all the key components are in place:

args.command:
echo "=== Services ==="
ps aux | grep -E "sshd|nginx" | grep -v grep | head -5

echo ""
echo "=== Git repo ==="
ls -la /git/
ls -la /git/project.git/hooks/post-receive

echo ""
echo "=== SSL ==="
ls -la /etc/nginx/ssl/

echo ""
echo "=== Nginx config ==="
cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/git-deploy

echo ""
echo "=== Git user ==="
id git
grep git /etc/passwd

tool_response.name: run_bash

parts.text:
The entire system is set up and verified. Here's a summary of what was configured:

1. **Git User**: Created a `git` user with password `password` and `git-shell` as the login shell
2. **Bare Git Repository**: Created at `/git/project.git` with a symlink at `/git/project`
3. **SSH Server**: Configured with password authentication enabled, running on port 22
4. **Post-receive Hook**: Deploys `main` branch to `/var/www/main/` and `dev` branch to `/var/www/dev/` using `git checkout -f`
5. **Nginx**: Configured with HTTPS (self-signed cert) on port 8443:
   - `/` serves from `/var/www/main/` (main branch)
   - `/dev/` serves from `/var/www/dev/` (dev branch)
6. **Self-signed SSL Certificate**: Generated for `localhost` at `/etc/nginx/ssl/`
